page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.large is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, providing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.g5.large in the lower performance tiers. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1101.29, and the multi-core score is 1453.18, both falling into the weak to poor categories. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 81.66 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 10.25 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write speed of 9.61 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks also reflect these limitations, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 673.28 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 18.92 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but modest processing and memory throughput, the ecs.g5.large represents a low-density hardware profile. It is suitable for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, which registers a static throughput of 1.31 GB/sec, or low-demand database operations, where it achieves 1.53 million Redis SET operations per second. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, and the 1 Gbps network baseline limits high-throughput data transfers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.large is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio with no hyperthreading. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1101.29 and a multi-core score of 1453.18, placing it in the lower performance percentiles. Memory bandwidth is also limited, reaching 10.25 GB/sec for large block reads. The ecs.g5.large is best suited for low-intensity workloads such as basic web serving, lightweight database tasks, or small-scale application hosting where dedicated CPU resources are required but high computational throughput is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.large is a general-purpose virtual server with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, designed for low-intensity workloads requiring dedicated resources.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.large is a general-purpose KVM instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with balanced memory allocation for entry-level, low-throughput general purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage included
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Suited for basic web serving and lightweight database operations
